title: moraea venenata
intro: Moraea venenata, also known as the poisonous moraea or yellow-flowered corm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Moraea venenata include its ability to treat a range of ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and analgesic properties. Herbal preparations of Moraea venenata can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
